description_long: This house features a prominent front porch with classical columns supporting a covered entryway, accessed by a wide staircase. The structure is two stories tall with an additional attic or partial third level, topped by a gable roof. The exterior walls are finished in a light color, with brick or stone accents at the base and around the porch. Windows are arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern, with some variation on the side elevations, and the main entry is centrally located. The house sits on a raised foundation, with steps leading up to the porch and side access points. The overall design suggests a blend of Colonial Revival and Neoclassical architectural influences, with a formal, stately appearance.
description: A stately, two-story house with a gable roof, prominent front porch with columns, and symmetrical window arrangement. The exterior is light-colored with brick or stone accents and a raised foundation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry with wide steps, a covered porch supported by columns, and symmetrical windows.
description_view_1: Left side view revealing the side staircase, additional windows, and the depth of the porch structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a simpler facade with fewer windows and no visible entryway.
description_view_3: Right side view highlighting the side porch access, multiple windows, and the raised foundation.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with wide steps, covered porch, classical columns, and a raised foundation
